fragility_points: Overshoot above 119 before noon ET is the main directional failure mode.
Public auditability of reply/main-feed and deleted-post treatment is incomplete from raw post rows.
If XTracker malfunctions, fallback counting on Truth Social could introduce settlement ambiguity.
key_assumptions: XTracker remains the governing and functional resolution source through noon ET.
Additional counted posts before resolution are unlikely to push the total above 119.
The current 103 count will not be materially revised downward by reclassification.
main_logical_chain: Check the contract and identify the governing settlement source as XTracker's Post Counter.
Verify the tracked account identity and exact market-linked time window on XTracker.
Observe that the official tracked total is already 103, inside the 100-119 band.
Discount confidence because the window is still open and raw captured-post rows do not map perfectly to the official counter.
Conclude lean Yes, but below the market's confidence level.
main_thesis: Lean Yes because XTracker, the governing resolution source, currently shows 103 counted Truth Social posts in the exact window, but confidence should be discounted for late-window overshoot risk and counting-rule opacity.
quote_anchors: XTracker Post Counter is the primary resolution source
replies do not count unless recorded on the main feed
source_quality_view: Good primary source quality because the tracker is the explicit settlement source and exposes public docs plus live endpoints, but independence is limited and rule-filter transparency is only partial.
strongest_disconfirmers: The market is still live, so a late posting burst could push the final official total above 119.
Raw captured posts endpoint returned 105 rows while the official counter showed 103, leaving some counting-rule opacity.
strongest_supports: XTracker stats endpoint shows totalBetweenStartAndEnd = 103 for the exact market-linked window.
XTracker user endpoint matches the tracked poster to verified Truth Social account realDonaldTrump.
Polymarket rules explicitly make XTracker's Post Counter the primary resolution source.
timing_relevance: High: the count is already in range but the market remains open until noon ET, so late-window posting can still flip the outcome.
unresolved_ambiguities: Which exact two captured posts were excluded from the official 103 count.
How much additional posting activity will occur before the deadline.
Whether any borderline reply or deleted-post cases emerge near resolution.
what_would_change_view: A tracker update toward or above 120, evidence of tracker malfunction, or proof that several currently counted posts should be excluded would move me materially away from Yes.
